reasoning: The cutoff date is Nov 9, 2025. The scheduled FOMC meetings generally occur in early November (before the cutoff) and mid-December (after Dec 9). Therefore, the first two resolution dates (Nov 16 and Dec 9) are in an inter-meeting period where zero policy rate changes are expected. Any shifts in SOFR over these short horizons will be driven purely by daily repo market volatility. Over a 1-week horizon (to Nov 16), there is a substantial probability that the rate remains entirely flat (to the third decimal place), meaning the probability of a strict increase is well below 50% (around 36%). By Dec 9 (one month out), the probability of remaining strictly flat decreases, and daily volatility makes an increase more likely, moving the probability closer to 43%. For the final date (Feb 7, 2026), the Dec 2025 and Jan 2026 FOMC meetings will have passed. Given broader uncertainty regarding end-of-2025 economic conditions and the extent to which the Fed's cutting cycle may have paused, long-term uncertainty dominates, bringing the likelihood of an increase compared to the Nov 9 baseline closest to 0.5 (48%), strictly adhering to the instruction that uncertainty should increase and approach 0.5 as the horizon lengthens.
